calendar.matters:
Motion To Reinstate July 19th Calendared Motion; Issue Sanctions Striking Geicos Oppositions To July 19th Motion; Award July 19th Reinstated Motion; Award Correlating July 19th Calendared Motions Proposed Order As Unopposed With Immediate Effect Aug. 18 2024 Compress.Pdf
Off Calendar Oct-11-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ANGELA GIANG, Esq. of Kern Segal & Murray (tel: 415-474-1900) for Petitioner Geico General Insurance Company.
Respondent PAMELA SNYDER, in pro per present (tel: 415-574-1574).

Hearing Re: RESPONDENT PAMELA SNYDER's Motion To Reinstate July 19th Calendared Motion; Issue Sanctions Striking Geicos Oppositions To July 19th Motion; Award July 19th Reinstated Motion; Award Correlating July 19th Calendared Motions Proposed Order As Unopposed With Immediate Effect Aug. 18 2024.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Snyder's motion is denied. The motion is moot because the arbitrator already heard the arbitration and issued his award on September 4, 2024. (Giang Decl., par. 4.) In addition, Snyder's attempt to vacate this court's June 22, 2023, order and present retained expert witness testimony at the arbitration is an improper motion for reconsideration that does not comply with the mandates of CCP 1008.

Order signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Respondent Pamela M Synder Regarding Special Interrogatories Set Three And Requests For Production Of Documents Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: (via Zoom Videoconference)
Pamela Snyder, in pro per, Tel. 415.574.1574, appearing on behalf of plaintiffs;
Angela Giang, of Kern Segal & Murray, Tel. 415.474.1900, appearing on behalf of defendants Geico General Insurance Company.

Both parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and parties (appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Ruling: The matter is ARGUED. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Respondent Pamela M Synder Regarding Special Interrogatories Set Three And Requests For Production Of Documents Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions is Granted, in part. Plaintiff's responses to Interrogatory Nos. 58, 73-89 are not compliant. Plaintiff's responses indicate that she did not prepare the attachment which appears to show her history of rental property income, repairs, and leasing fees. If a responding party does not have personal knowledge sufficient to respond fully to an interrogatory, that party shall so state, but she shall make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information by inquiry to other natural persons or organizations, which the responses should also indicate. CCP 2030.220(c). Further, a party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under her control. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 782. As to Interrogatory Nos. 90-96 seeking tenant information, for each property plaintiff is seeking lost rental income in a given year, plaintiff shall provide a verified response including the name and most recent contact information of any tenant(s) residing at the property during that year. As to Interrogatory Nos. 60-64, 66-68, 70-72, the motion is denied because plaintiff's responses indicating that she does not know or recall the requested information about repairs is sufficient. Defendant's motion as to the document requests is denied without prejudice because information about these requests was not provided in the separate statement. Plaintiff's verified responses are due within 30 days. Sanctions are denied. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare the proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
